mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate indicates an accommodative monetary policy stance, which reduces the discount rate applied to equity cash flows and supports higher stock prices over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward 60-day Rank IC of month-over-month FEDFUNDS change remains below 0.03 in absolute value or the long-short portfolio fails to produce positive Sharpe ratio over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate above 5.5% signals labor market weakness and rising recession risk, which reduces corporate earnings expectations and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward return following UNRATE crossing above 5.5% is not significantly negative (mean return < 0) or if the trading rule fails to generate a positive risk-adjusted return over a 2-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in housing starts and industrial production indicate a broad-based economic contraction, which lowers aggregate demand and corporate profits, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: Both HOUST and INDPRO decline month-over-month for at least 1 month
failure_condition: If the average 20-day forward return in months where both series decline is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or if the signal generates a negative Sharpe ratio in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: In a moderate inflation environment (CPI between 210 and 220), an increase in nonfarm payrolls signals real economic strength without overheating, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward return following positive PAYEMS changes when CPI is in the 210-220 range is not significantly positive (mean > 0) or if the signal fails in walk-forward tests when the regime condition holds.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production for two consecutive months signals weakening manufacturing activity and economic contraction, which reduces earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the average 60-day forward return following two-month INDPRO declines is not significantly negative, or if the Rank IC of INDPRO change is not negative at the 60-day horizon with p-value > 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 3% and housing starts are below 1000 thousand units, the combination signals that aggressive monetary easing has not revived the housing market, indicating persistent distress and negative equity returns over the next 20 days.
regime_hint: housing crisis with failed monetary transmission
failure_condition: If the average 20-day forward return when both conditions hold is not negative (mean > 0) or the signal provides no predictive power over baseline in a decision tree model.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in real retail sales (RSAFS) reflects strong consumer spending, which drives corporate revenues and stock prices higher over the short term of 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward return Rank IC of month-over-month RSAFS change is not positive and significant (IC > 0.03) in walk-forward testing, or if the signal fails to produce positive returns in a long-short portfolio.

mechanism_narrative: In a high unemployment regime (UNRATE above 6%), rising inflation (CPI) signals stagflationary pressures, which erode real earnings and lead to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward return when UNRATE > 6% and CPIAUCSL increases is not significantly negative (mean return > -1%) or if the trading rule does not outperform a simple buy-and-hold during high unemployment periods.
